mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy economy, leading to positive consumer sentiment and higher corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive PAYEMS growth over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) drops significantly, it signals aggressive monetary easing to stimulate the economy, which typically boosts equity valuations as discount rates fall and liquidity increases, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS falling by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and purchasing power, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of periods where both RSAFS 3-month growth is strong (>0.5%) and UNRATE is low (<5.0%).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals worsening economic conditions and reduced future construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1000 (thousands of units)
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during economic contractions, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, signaling reduced corporate earnings and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods following a month-over-month INDPRO decline exceeding 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL) allows the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative monetary policy, which supports higher equity valuations by keeping borrowing costs low and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth is consistently below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with a falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a deteriorating labor market prompting monetary easing, which initially signals economic weakness but eventually supports equity markets as policy stimulus takes effect, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS falls by > 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises by > 0.5pp and FEDFUNDS falls by > 0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a strengthening economy, leading to improved corporate earnings expectations and increased investor confidence, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods when GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3.0%.
